data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-09T01:46:18.460Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically regarding synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known breaking change in EF Core. Let me recall what I know:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations were never natively supported - they were always implemented as sync-over-async wrappers. In EF Core 10 (.NET 10), there was an opt-in mechanism to allow this pattern. In EF Core 11 (.NET 11), the sync-over-async APIs for the Cosmos DB provider are being removed entirely.


Given the current date is May 2026, this is a hypothetical scenario since .NET 10 and 11 haven't released yet, but the breaking change around removing synchronous support from the Cosmos DB provider is a real planned change. The core issue is that the Cosmos DB provider never truly supported synchronous operations—it always wrapped async calls internally, and that workaround is being eliminated. In .NET 11, that opt-in gets removed completely, so any synchronous LINQ or SaveChanges calls will immediately throw an exception at runtime. The only solution is converting everything to async methods like `ToListAsync()`, `FirstAsync()`, `CountAsync()`, and `SaveChangesAsync()`.
